title: achillea atrata subsp. halleri
intro: Achillea atrata subsp. halleri, also known as Alpine Yarrow or Haller's Yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and delicate white or pink flowers. Traditionally, Alpine Yarrow has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils, including camphor, borneol, and borneol acetate. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and easing respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Alpine Yarrow can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
